title: "Seagrass" eggs collector for common cuttlefish
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3h9af","text":"This protocol refers to a study having the aim to identify and test at field artificial structures suitable as collectors for common cuttlefish Sepia officinalis eggs in wild condition.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5v36","text":"During the same study, three different protocols were tested and compared. This one refers to the named 'seagrass' collector protocol, due to their similarity to the natural seagrass meadaw.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"772f2","text":"This work was supported by the project “Protection, improvement and integrated management of the sea environment and of cross-border natural resources – ECOSEA”, funded by EU Adriatic IPA Cross-border Cooperation 2007-2013 (2°ord./0236/0).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4pm86","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1t8kr","text":"A total of 160 collectors were installed in the vertices of an electro-galvanized iron mesh having sheet size 2000 x 3000 cm, 15 x 15 cm mesh dimension and 0.6 cm diameter. The iron mesh was installed in a tubular galvanized frame of 4.83 cm diameter. A single eggs collector consist of 50 cm segments of polyethylene floating rope having a diameter of 0.8 cm. Each modulus has been installed on the seafloor at the beginning of the spawning period of the cuttlefish in a linear composition of 9 'seagrass' collectors. The modules have been tested in two central Adriatic Sea areas (Senigallia and Portonovo, Italy) where trawling is forbidden (by low and by a presence of anti-trawling structures). The structures were fixed on the seabed close to the artificial reefs modules in order to protect them from heavy storms.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e1iui","text":"This work was supported by the project “Protection, improvement and integrated management of the sea environment and of cross-border natural resources – ECOSEA”, funded by EU Adriatic IPA Cross-border Cooperation 2007-2013 before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"eeshu","text":"An adequate vessel for transshipment/positioning and scuba divers are needed for a proper installation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
